title: dichrocephala chrysanthemifolia var. chrysanthemifolia
intro: Dichrocephala chrysanthemifolia var. chrysanthemifolia, also known as the Yellow Flowered Dichrocephala or Yellow Buttonbush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving cough and cold symptoms, and supporting respiratory health. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.
